Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 12:36 - Engineers are on their way. At this point we think the issue is a fault with equipment in your local substation in the St. Johns Lye area. We didn't know this was going to happen, so time frames we provide throughout are estimated and are subject to change.

Our estimated time to have power back on is currently between 16:00 and 17:00, but after we arrive and assess the situation this may change. The reason for this is there can sometimes be a more complex underlying issue found by our engineer's tests which may take longer, and if not we may get power back on sooner.

All of our texts are written and sent personally by our team, and we'll let you know once our staff arrive.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 12:34 - Our engineers have restored power to all 185 properties in the Rusper Road area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have diverted power around the overhead network fault.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hi, it's Adam from the messaging team at UK Power Networks. I understand how difficult it can be without power and the additional worries this can bring.

For both our business customers and those at home, we will work as quickly and safely as possible to restore supplies. If you need any additional support or have any concerns, please call us on 105 or tweet us @ukpowernetworks.

I will personally continue to keep you updated with our progress.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 11:37 - We have now heard from our staff on site and additional time is needed to allow them to locate the fault on our overhead network.

If this fails, we have no option but to try and locate the cause of the fault, and this may involve a cable repair. This can take longer due to needing specialist teams.

Complications can occur sometimes, so time frames are subject to change, but we are now estimating we'll restore power between now & 12:30. We will give you the latest when our staff can safely stop work and update us.
